pages.raw_text:
The council last night showed a
disposition to make the railroads
come through with concessions be-
fore the franchises now on file for
approval are granted. At commit
tee of the whole meeting last night
protests came thick and fast,
making Roadway impossible until
clearer plans are secured. The
North Coast and the Great North
ern applications for lines west of
the city were chiefly discussed,
and it was finally decided to have
plan covering the proposed lines
of both companies prepared in or
der that the council could get some
idea as to what was wanted.


A committee consisting of Lam
bert, Schiller, Dalke, Engineer Raj
ston and Corporation Counsel Allen
will meet with property owners
and the railroad officials at the
Whittier school April II.


The council has come to the con-
clusion that the railroads want ev-
erything they can get and show
no disposition to pay for anything
or provide suitable street cross
ings.


The application Of the Great
Northern to close streets between
Fourteenth avenue and Greenwood
cemetery will cause a hot fight,
and the North Coast application to
close streets in the western part
of the city has met with protests.


The protests against the 1de
avenue plan of the North Coast
was made a special order of bust
ness for April 20 and the applica-
ton of Spokane, Columbia & West
ern, was set for April %.


The railroad officials who took
part in the discussion last night in
cluded L. r. Hamblen of the North
Coast and l.. c. Gilman and Will
lam Bowen of the Great Northern.
